parsed_review.summary: CP00-549 is a clean descriptor-only AI-governance pack (RP17.P09.M05.S19-RP17.P09.M06.S06, risk A, 10 units) and its descriptor-verified production_ready candidate claim is fully supported. Pack binding, deliverable distribution {implementation:3,hermes_evidence:1,claude_review:2,security_audit:2,test:1,ui:1}, and micro-title split {Permission And Audit Binding:4,Synthetic Fixture Set:6} match across registry binding, manifest, closeout-pack-plan entry, baseline, and contract current_pack. All 7 required public exports exist (create/validate Cp549 descriptor+caseSet in service.js; coverage, slice validator, Hermes/Claude packet, and closeout handoff in validators.js) and are re-exported via the index.js barrel. Independent inspection confirms descriptor-only/no-write boundaries: the source performs no fetch/fs/child_process/network/DB operations, imports are intra-package only, and every generator emits frozen plain-object descriptors with comprehensive no-real-data, no-runtime-dispatch, no-audit-write, and no-leak attestations. Authority boundary holds — Claude is read-only and explicitly not final approver (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true), and local validation does not claim enterprise trust. Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic review packet/receipt shapes (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime. The validator deep-equals the contract projection (descriptor, requirements, no-write attestation) and asserts the historical binding tail is CP00-549; tests assert coverage, descriptor-only rows, authority boundaries, packets, handoff, and the claude-opus-4-8/read-only review constraints. The readiness auth-preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON, so no CP178+ invalid-evidence rejection applies. production_ready and runtime_ready remain false, correctly avoiding any runtime over-claim. Unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook docs, docx, ldip result) are correctly classified out of pack scope. No P0/P1/P2 issues found. One P3 informational note: the three post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present, which is expected at this read-only review step and must be generated before the gated final closeout-pack:validate and commit.

findings.title: Post-review evidence artifacts not yet present (expected at this review stage)
findings.details: manifest.required_pack_artifacts lists docs/closeout-packs/cp00-549/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist on disk. This is consistent with manifest.pack_level_claude_review.hardening_sequence, which places normalize, receipt_validate, finding_adjudication, and construction_inspection AFTER the read_only_claude_run_raw_capture step (this review). command-evidence.json notes also confirm the post-review gated commands (closeout-pack:review:run/normalize/receipt:validate, closeout-pack:validate, git diff --check --cached) run before commit. Therefore the absence is expected and non-blocking, not an implementation or boundary defect.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the normalize -> receipt_validate -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final_closeout_validation steps generate all three artifacts from this accepted review before running the gated closeout-pack:validate and creating the commit; do not commit with required_pack_artifacts missing.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; expected state at the read-only review step per the documented hardening sequence. No code, evidence, or authority-boundary defect. Does not block pack or goal closeout.
